more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Court orders seizure of Fadzayi Mahere’s property over US$33k legal costs in Minister Machakaire defamation case
more.description: The High Court has issued a writ authorising the seizure of property belonging to lawyer and opposition politician Fadzayi Mahere to recover US$33,395 in legal costs owed to Youth Empowerment minister Tinoda Machakaire following a failed special plea in a defamation case. The writ of execution, issued under case number HCH 3394/25, directs the Sheriff [...]The post Court orders seizure of Fadzayi Mahere’s property over US$33k legal costs in Minister Machakaire defamation case appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Zimbabweans stranded in UAE due to airspace closures exempted from overstay fines
more.description: THE government has announced that Zimbabwean nationals stranded in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) due to recent airspace closures and suspended flights will not be required to pay overstay fines. In an advisory issued by the Zimbabwean Embassy in Abu Dhabi, authorities said the exemption from penalties took effect on February 28 following confirmation from [...]The post Zimbabweans stranded in UAE due to airspace closures exempted from overstay fines appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: High Court frees ‘Madzibaba Ishmael’ on bail pending appeal in rape case
more.description: Controversial religious leader Ishmael Chokurongerwa, popularly known as Madzibaba Ishmael, has been freed pending appeal after the High Court ruled that his case raised serious legal questions that could overturn his conviction. In a ruling delivered at the High Court of Zimbabwe in Harare on March 4, Justice Neville Wamambo granted Chokurongerwa bail pending appeal [...]The post High Court frees ‘Madzibaba Ishmael’ on bail pending appeal in rape case appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Women’s rights at risk under proposed constitutional amendments – ERC
more.description: PROPOSED constitutional amendments in Zimbabwe could weaken institutional safeguards meant to advance gender equality and women’s political participation, the Election Resource Centre (ERC Africa) has said. Under the recently gazetted Constitutional Amendment Bill, Zimbabwe’s independent commissions may be restructured by removing the Gender Commission as a standalone constitutional body and placing its responsibilities under the Zimbabwe [...]The post Women’s rights at risk under proposed constitutional amendments – ERC appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Court orders urgent hearing in Telecontract–Econet network clash
more.description: The High Court has ruled that a dispute between telecommunications firms Telecontract (Pvt) Ltd and Econet Wireless (Pvt) Ltd over the blocking of network traffic must be treated as urgent after Telecontract said the disruptions were causing revenue losses and threatening its business operations. In a judgment delivered in the Commercial Division in Harare, Justice [...]The post Court orders urgent hearing in Telecontract–Econet network clash appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Ibhetshu LikaZulu withdraws ConCourt challenge, cites “unrelenting” attacks
more.description: Civic organisation Ibhetshu LikaZulu has withdrawn its Constitutional Court challenge against the government’s proposed Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Bill, which is linked to the controversial 2030 agenda seeking to extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s tenure.The post Ibhetshu LikaZulu withdraws ConCourt challenge, cites “unrelenting” attacks appeared first on Nehanda Radio.

more.title: Lawyers Keep USD450 000 Legal Fees
more.description: Source: Lawyers Keep USD450 000 Legal Fees Lawyers involved in Matabeleland pressure group Ibhetshu LikaZulu and secretary-general Mbuso Fuzwayo’s Constitutional Court bid to block President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term extension will keep their staggering legal fees involving US$450 000 despite their client withdrawing the case in a shock move this afternoon, an insider said. Fuzwayo has [...]The post Lawyers Keep USD450 000 Legal Fees appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.description: Source: Jonathan Moyo Reacts To Ibhetshu LikaZulu Move Exiled former minister and MP Jonathan Moyo, now a ruling Zanu PF political strategist, has replied to questions sent to him by The NewsHawks on Ibhetshu LikaZulu and its secretary-general Mbuso Fuzwayo’s shock withdrawal of a Constitutional Court application challenging President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term extension and a [...]The post Jonathan Moyo Reacts To Ibhetshu LikaZulu Move appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: VP Chiwenga’s Wife Minnie Baloyi Removed From Military Intelligence In Soft Demotion
more.description: VP Chiwenga’s Wife, Minnie Baloyi, Removed From Army Intelligence In Soft Demotion Zimbabwe’s Second Lady, Colonel Miniyothabo “Minnie” Baloyi, the wife of Vice President Constantino Chiwenga, has reportedly been removed from her position in the Zimbabwe National Army’s military intelligence directorate and reassigned to the Commander’s Pool, a unit where officers are stationed without specific [...]The post VP Chiwenga’s Wife Minnie Baloyi Removed From Military Intelligence In Soft Demotion appeared first on iHarare News.
